title: AI Agents: Why Building Them Is Way Harder Than You Think! #shorts
summary: Cyril Imhof argues that developing AI agents is harder than commonly perceived. The difficulty stems from the iterative, data-driven nature of AI requiring a fundamentally different engineering mindset and skillset compared to traditional software development.
writeup: In this short, Cyril Imhof makes the case that building AI agents is harder than commonly assumed. The core argument: agents are iterative and data-driven, so they demand a different engineering mindset and skillset than traditional software development, where deterministic logic and conventional specs are sufficient. The video frames spec-driven development as relevant to closing that gap. The takeaway is that treating agent development like ordinary software underestimates the work involved.
